---
title: "R for Management Consultants"
subtitle: "A Practical Introduction to R Programming for Global Health Professionals"
---

## Course Overview

This course teaches R programming from scratch, tailored for management consultants working in global health. Across 9 chapters, you will build a complete data analysis toolkit — from writing your first line of R code to producing publication-quality charts and reproducible reports for stakeholders.

::: {.callout-box}
**The central message:** R is not just for statisticians. It is a **practical tool** that helps consultants answer the questions that matter most: *What does the data say? How confident are we? Can we reproduce this analysis next quarter?*
:::

## The Consultant's R Workflow

By the end of this course, you will be able to follow this data-driven workflow:

1. **Import** facility-level and program data from CSV and Excel files (Chapter 3)
2. **Transform** raw data into analysis-ready tables with filtering, grouping, and summaries (Chapters 4--5)
3. **Visualize** patterns, trends, and comparisons with publication-quality charts (Chapters 6--7)
4. **Analyze** key indicators with descriptive statistics and simple regression (Chapter 8)
5. **Communicate** findings in reproducible Quarto reports for donors and program managers (Chapter 9)

## Chapters

### Part I: Getting Started

| Chapter | Title | Focus |
|---------|-------|-------|
| 1 | [Why R for Management Consultants](chapters/ch01.qmd) | Motivation, installing R + RStudio, first commands |
| 2 | [R Building Blocks](chapters/ch02.qmd) | Vectors, data types, functions, packages |

### Part II: Working with Data

| Chapter | Title | Focus |
|---------|-------|-------|
| 3 | [Importing & Exploring Data](chapters/ch03.qmd) | read_csv, tibbles, summary, handling missing values |
| 4 | [Data Transformation with dplyr](chapters/ch04.qmd) | Pipes, filter, select, mutate, summarise, group_by |
| 5 | [Reshaping & Joining Datasets](chapters/ch05.qmd) | pivot_longer/wider, joins, string basics |

### Part III: Visualization & Analysis

| Chapter | Title | Focus |
|---------|-------|-------|
| 6 | [Data Visualization with ggplot2](chapters/ch06.qmd) | Grammar of graphics, geoms, aesthetics, basic plots |
| 7 | [Publication-Quality Charts](chapters/ch07.qmd) | Themes, faceting, multi-panel layouts, exporting |
| 8 | [Descriptive Statistics for Consultants](chapters/ch08.qmd) | Means, proportions, confidence intervals, regression basics |

### Part IV: Communication

| Chapter | Title | Focus |
|---------|-------|-------|
| 9 | [Reproducible Reports with Quarto](chapters/ch09.qmd) | Code chunks, inline R, rendering to HTML/PDF/DOCX |

## The Complete Toolkit

| Chapters | Skill |
|----------|-------|
| 1--2 | R fundamentals & environment |
| 3--5 | Data import, transformation & reshaping |
| 6--7 | Data visualization |
| 8 | Descriptive statistics & regression |
| 9 | Reproducible reporting |

## Prerequisites

- No prior programming experience required
- Basic familiarity with spreadsheets (Excel or Google Sheets)
- See the [R Setup](r-setup.qmd) page for installation instructions

## Connection to Inference and Intervention

This course is designed as a companion to the [Inference and Intervention](../inference-and-intervention/index.qmd) course on causal models for MNH program analysis. The R skills taught here provide the foundation for the R code workshops in that course, including data visualization with ggplot2, data manipulation with dplyr, and statistical analysis.

::: {.callout-box .green}
**For MNH program analysts:** This toolkit enables you to move beyond Excel-based reporting toward reproducible, transparent analyses that stakeholders and donors can trust — and that you can update in minutes, not days.
:::
